scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a normal grocery availability and replenishment summary only when item master, POS, inventory, backroom, ASN, vendor, and promotion records aligned.
The lane recognized a required supply shortage, preserved reserved and inbound evidence, and escalated promotion and customer-impact risk without overstating availability.
The lane converted a vendor allocation cut into an action note and routed substitute-item approval outside the lane.
The lane refused to release temperature-held seafood to saleable inventory and escalated safety authority.
Conflicting receiving, traceability lot, inventory, shelf, and POS records blocked the availability claim until authoritative evidence is retrieved.
The lane kept stockout and substitution evidence intact while routing refund, pricing, and policy exceptions to authorized owners.
High-volume online pick exceptions were triaged by evidence completeness, customer impact, safety posture, and owner without skipping item-level checks.
Category margin and promotional markdown authority were rejected as adjacent pricing or merchandising ownership.
Missing tenant facts, approved supplier, reorder rules, and retrieval hooks produced a blocked prerequisite list rather than guessed replenishment guidance.
Baby formula stockout, suspicious account, refund, and order-spike signals were escalated for fraud and customer-harm review without in-lane adjudication.
Conflicting public, local, and tenant traceability or recall rules were recorded as a human-resolution blocker with hold posture preserved.
Tenant substitution rules were applied only where documented while preserving public safety, consumer-protection, and approval boundaries.
The lane handled grocery terminology and artifact shapes across POS, OMS, WMS, vendor, and inventory records without flattening into generic stock language.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard profile and 0.92 minimum pass-rate threshold.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared abilities, supply-procurement-and-availability abilities, and retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim a retained implementation.
